PATIENT DISCHARGE SUMMARY

67 years

November 28, 2025

Service:

Internal Medicine

Insurance:

Medicare Part A

Room:

4-West, Bed 2

DIAGNOSES

Medicare Part A

Room:

4-West, Bed 2

DIAGNOSES

Primary: Community-Acquired Pneumonia (ICD-10: J18.9) Secondary: (cid:127) Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, uncontrolled (E11.65) (cid:127) Hypertension, essential (I10) (cid:127) Chronic Kidney Disease, Stage 3a (N18.31) (cid:127) Hyperlipidemia (E78.5) (cid:127) Obesity, BMI 34.2 (E66.01)

HOSPITAL COURSE

Mr. Anderson is a 67-year-old male with a past medical history significant for Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, CKD Stage 3a, and Hyperlipidemia who presented to the Emergency Department on November 28, 2025, with a 4-day history of productive cough with yellow-green sputum, fever (Tmax 102.4°F), shortness of breath on exertion, and general malaise. Chest X-ray revealed right lower lobe consolidation consistent with pneumonia. Initial labs showed WBC 14,200/mL with left shift, CRP 12.8 mg/dL, procalcitonin
with left shift, CRP 12.8 mg/dL, procalcitonin 2.3 ng/mL, and creatinine 1.8 mg/dL (baseline 1.4).

The patient was started on IV ceftriaxone 1g daily and oral azithromycin 500mg daily per CAP guidelines. Blood cultures drawn prior to antibiotics grew no organisms after 5 days. Sputum culture grew Streptococcus pneumoniae sensitive to ceftriaxone. Supplemental oxygen was provided via nasal cannula at 2-4 L/min for the first 72 hours. The patient's diabetes was managed with sliding scale insulin; his home metformin was held due to acute kidney injury. Creatinine peaked at 2.1 on hospital day 3, then
Creatinine peaked at 2.1 on hospital day 3, then trended down to 1.6 by discharge with IV fluid hydration.

By hospital day 7, the patient was afebrile for >48 hours, WBC normalized to 8,400/mL, CRP decreased to 2.1, oxygen saturation was 96% on room air, and he was tolerating oral intake well. He was transitioned to oral levofloxacin to complete a 7-day total antibiotic course. Physical therapy assessed his mobility and recommended home exercises. Social work arranged a follow-up appointment.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS

Medication

Dose/Route

Instructions

Levofloxacin

500mg PO daily

3 days remaining

Metformin

500mg PO daily

3 days remaining

Metformin

1000mg PO BID

Resume when creatinine < 1.5

Lisinopril

20mg PO daily

Continue home dose

Atorvastatin

40mg PO at bedtime

Continue home dose

Amlodipine

5mg PO daily

Continue home dose

Albuterol inhaler

2 puffs Q4H PRN

New prescription

Acetaminophen

650mg PO Q6H PRN

For fever or pain

FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS

For fever or pain

FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS

1. PCP follow-up with Dr. Michael Torres within 7 days of discharge. 2. Repeat chest X-ray in 6 weeks to confirm resolution. 3. BMP and creatinine check in 5 days (lab order placed). 4. Return to ED if: fever > 101°F, worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, hemoptysis, or inability to take oral medications. 5. Pneumococcal vaccination (PCV20) recommended at follow-up visit.
